Unloads FTDI USB drivers on MacOS 10.9 to prevent reverting to original Apple drivers after reboot.

# Purpose
This script is a shell script designed to address a specific issue in MacOS 10.9 (Mavericks) related to USB FTDI drivers. It unloads the Apple USB FTDI driver and the FTDI USB Serial Driver from the system. The script uses the `sudo kextunload` command to remove these kernel extensions, which helps prevent the system from reverting to the original Apple USB FTDI drivers after a reboot. This script provides narrow functionality, focusing on driver management for a specific operating system version.
